description: Data that does not change value from one transaction to another. Considered for reference values that should not or rarely change.
allowed_values: A numeric, alphabetic, or alphanumeric value that rarely if ever changes.

description: Specifically about currency; a quantity of money – a monetary amount.
allowed_values: Any valid monetary amount value.

description: An arithmetic value or quantity (including fractions and units of measure) of anything except money. Could also be used in or be the result of a mathematical computation like an average, minimum, maximum, etc.
allowed_values: Any valid numeric value or decimal value.

description: An arithmetic numeric expression representing a rate or proportion per hundred (part divided by whole).
allowed_values: Typical stored decimal values range between 0 and +1 at any level of precision. May be negative or exceed +1 in specific cases.

description: An arithmetic quotient representing the quantitative relationship between exactly two distinct quantities, calculated as one quantity divided by another without reference to a whole or total.
allowed_values: Stored as a single decimal value ranging from 0 to any positive number. Negative values possible only if one quantity is negative.

description: Formatted data which can contain several data elements with defined contents and structure and must be interpreted before use by a system or application.
allowed_values: Not applicable.

description: A digital container of data that represents images, sound, video executables, etc., typically identified by a computer file extension.
allowed_values: Not applicable.

description: Data having undefined content and/or unstructured format with no definable allowed values; free-form description or comment that does not specifically identify an object.
allowed_values: Not applicable.

description: A value limited to four different status types regarding the existence or presence of a situation or condition.
allowed_values: One of the following literal values: "Yes", "No", "Unknown", "NotApplicable".

description: A boolean using a datatype of bit or byte. Typically stored physically as 0 or 1 (some DBMS may allow NULL).
allowed_values: One of the following Boolean values: "0", "1", or NULL (DBMS-dependent).

description: A short alphanumeric value that represents a longer, fuller name or description of an object, thing, or classification belonging to a defined set.
allowed_values: Defined allowed values, usually in a set.

description: Meaningful alphabetic data using words which identifies a specific object or may identify a type or category of an object or thing.
allowed_values: Allowed but optional.

description: A meaningless value (often sequentially or randomly generated) that uniquely represents an occurrence of a record about an object, entity, or thing.
allowed_values: Not applicable.

description: Alphanumeric data not using words which identifies a specific object or thing, but can also identify a type or category of an object or thing.
allowed_values: Not applicable.

description: Actual calendar date.
allowed_values: Any valid date value.

description: Actual clock time, expressed in hours, minutes, and sometimes seconds.
allowed_values: Any valid time value.

description: Hybrid DATE/TIME classword combination.
allowed_values: Any valid timestamp value.

description: A spatial geometric reference that defines the intersection of two coordinate lines.
allowed_values: No distinct set of allowed values.

description: A spatial geometric shape that defines a straight connection between two points or may extend infinitely in both directions.
allowed_values: No distinct set of allowed values.

description: A spatial geometric shape that defines a multi-sided geographic area.
allowed_values: No distinct set of allowed values.
